title: Ante-Endor Association
content:

{{Quote|It's time we all put an end to the fable of Endor! Come here and listen to the truth.|AEA speaker|<ref name="TPA" />}}
The '''Ante-Endor Association''', or '''AEA''' for short, was a pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] organization based on [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]] that claimed that the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] never occurred and was a piece of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]]. The group insisted that the Empire was still operating at full strength, further claiming that the Rebels had been responsible for the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], while testing a weapon to rival the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. The group also declared that there were no more [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], referring to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. The AEA was led by none other than [[Gade Yedan]], the assistant to [[Gyr Keela]], President of the [[Mrlsst Trade and Science Academy]]. When the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Loka Hask]] came to Mrlsst during negotiations for [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking technology]], he made the members of the AEA his underlings, using them to steal precious [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] and frame the [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], who represented the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in said negotiations.

==Description==
{{Quote|How stupid do they think we are?! Any sentient wouldn't swallow that the ultimate in Imperial technology could be destroyed by the same bunch that had blown up Alderaan trying to invent some weapon to match the original Death Star!|An AEA speaker|<ref name="TPA" />}}
The Ante-Endor Association's raison d'être was to contest the very existence of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="TPA" /> during which the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was defeated by the enemy [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]; not only did the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] manage to destroy the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]]-sized [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], but [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the battle by his own second-in-command, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="Episode VI">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

The members of the AEA described the Alliance's account of the battle as a mere fable, putting forth their own revisionist version of the events. According to the AEA, the Rebels were too much of a ragtag army to be actually threatening to the second Death Star, a most striking symbol of the Empire's technological might. They also claimed that, a few years earlier, the Alliance [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] had blown up the planet of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] while trying to invent a superweapon to match the Empire's original Death Star.<ref name="TPA" /> In fact, Alderaan had been [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] at the hands of the Imperial [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], who had used the first Death Star's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] on the planet.<ref name="Episode IV">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

[[File:LukeClimbsXWing-ANHOfficialPix.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Luke Skywalker, a major member of the Rebel Alliance whose existence was disputed by AEA propaganda]]
The AEA also contested the claim that a Jedi was involved in the Battle of Endor, arguing that those ancient warriors did not exist anymore.<ref name="TPA" /> Indeed, most Jedi had been systematically killed as part of Emperor Palpatine's [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], which he had initiated immediately his creation of the Galactic Empire, in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. In order to justify that slaughter, Palpatine had convinced the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Senate]] that the Jedi had tried to assassinate him, which made their whole [[Jedi Order/Legends|order]] traitors to the state. Despite that, the Rebel Alliance did have a Jedi in his ranks, the young [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who had been trained by two of the Great Purge's rare survivors, [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].<ref name="Episode III">{{Film|III}}</ref>

While the AEA had their own way of negating the Rebel Alliance's victory at Endor, stating that the Emperor was still alive and that the Death Star was never destroyed, their version<ref name="TPA" /> did not coincide with the official propaganda shown at the [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Imperial Museum]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. That propaganda suggested that the Death Stars had been nothing but benign Planetary Ore Extractors, and that the Alliance had stolen the second of those to use it on inhabited planets. The museum's version did not deny that the Emperor had died, although suggested that he had infiltrated the ore extractor to offer the Rebels his forgiveness and his hand in friendship. However, his offer was supposedly rejected, and the Emperor was forced to destroy this Death Star himself, perishing in the process but allowing his citizens to live on. The same story also added that Darth Vader was slain in service to his Master.<ref name="Wedge's Gamble">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>

As a symbol of their organization, the AEA members used a variant of the six-spoked [[Imperial crest/Legends|Imperial crest]], which was itself a deformed version of the [[Order of Dai Bendu]]'s [[Republic crest/Legends|Galactic Roundel]]. The AEA's version had a stylized Death Star superimposed on the right side of the crest. On the association's banner, the modified crest was inserted in a golden, diamond-shaped figure, which was itself placed on a crimson background.<ref name="TPA" />

The members of the AEA wore uniforms that resembled those of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], comprising a double-breasted tunic with a choke collar, matching flared trousers, a pair of [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and gauntlet style [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. That outfit was entirely black with the exception of a broad golden trim that extended across the chest on one side and across the midsection on the other. The upper part of each sleeve was also decorate with a similar golden armband. The AEA's crest was featured on the armbands and at the front center of the chest.<ref name="TPA" />

==History==
{{Quote|How could it happen? How could you let slip the datacards through your fingers?!|Captain Loka Hask, to the members of the AEA|<ref name="TPA" />}}
The Ante-Endor Association emerged on the planet of [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], within the two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] that followed the Battle of Endor. The AEA held meetings on the university campus, claiming to enlighten students with the truth about Endor. As the [[Mrlssi]] prided themselves on freedom of speech, no matter who spoke, nothing could prevent the spread of the AEA's propaganda. Besides, a majority of teachers of the Mrlsst academies supported the Empire at the time. The AEA was headed by [[Gade Yedan]], a [[Myke]] who also served as the assistant to [[Gyr Keela]], President of the [[Mrlsst Trade and Science Academy]].<ref name="TPA" />

At that time, the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Loka Hask]] came to Mrrlst in hopes of finding new [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking technology]] from the [[Mrlssi]], who had put up for bids their [[Phantom Project]] to the [[Imperial Remnant|last holdings]] of the Empire and the burgeoning [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], born from the Rebel Alliance. The New Republic's representatives were the members of the [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], led by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Several members of the Rogue Squadron, namely [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]], [[Isplourrdacartha Estillo|Plourr Ilo]], and [[Dllr Nep]], came across a speech being given by the association. Celchu, who had fought in the Battle of Endor, was particularly aghast at their propaganda, and decided to confront the group publicly. The AEA speakers shrugged off Celchu's argument, making a fool of him in front of the assembled students, and the Rogue pilot left the premises in a foul mood. Later that evening, the AEA members waylaid Celchu as he wandered on the campus. As he continued to insist that the Battle of Endor had occurred as the Alliance recounted, they began showering him with blows. However, Celchu still managed to defeat all members of the group but one, who struck him from behind. The Rebel was only saved by the arrival of what appeared to be the [[Force ghost/Legends|ghost]] of a Jedi, who struck the final attacker and then vanished.<ref name="TPA" />

Shortly thereafter, the AEA members stole the [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] containing the plans for the Phantom Project and doctored the surveillance footage of their heist in order to implicate the Rogue Squadron pilots. However, the Endor negationists lost the datacards to a group of [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]] belonging to the band [[Ghost Jedi]]. Later, at least one member of the AEA, namely Gade Yedan, accompanied Captain Hask when he raided a [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Rorax Falken]]'s [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Falken's Lab|laboratory]] to steal the technology behind the secret [[gravitic polarization beam]] weapon. Yedan, however, was shot by Hask shortly before Falken's asteroid laboratory was consumed by a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Wormhole/Legends|wormhole]].<ref name="TPA" />
